question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that HashMultimap.create() allows duplicate values for the same key, by injecting duplicate StreamFileSignature objects for the same node and fileHash, causing consensusCount to be inflated and setStatus(CONSENSUS_REACHED) to be called multiple times on the same signature object?
